AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CRAIG MILL ROAD AND POWERS ROAD IN LUNENBURG COUNTY. MAXIMUM WINDS OF 75 MPH. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR THE TOPS WERE SNAPPED.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF0 TORNADO. MANY HARDWOOD TREES WERE FOUND TO BE UPROOTED AND/OR SNAPPED ALONG BRADBURY ROAD (STATE ROAD 672) AND MOSELEY ROAD (STATE ROAD 605). SEVERAL TREES ALONG THIS ROUTE WERE DOWNED ONTO HOMES. PATH LENGTH IS MEASURED AT 0.4 MILES, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 50 TO 75 YARDS. WIND INTENSITY IS ESTIMATED AT BETWEEN 75 AND 85 MILES PER HOUR.

NWS STORM SURVEY HAS CONFIRMED DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF0 TORNADO. TORNADO TRACK WAS A DISCONTINUOUS 8 MILE PATH FROM JUST EAST OF CAMDEN ALONG THE PASQUOTANK RIVER, CROSSING HIGHWAY 158 BEFORE LIFTING JUST WEST OF SHAWBORO. MINOR DAMAGE TO A FEW HOUSES WAS NOTED AT THE INTERSECTION OF BELCROSS RD AND PINCH GUT RD, BUT NO MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS SEEN. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SHEARED OR SNAPPED ALONG THE TRACK. ESTIMATED WINDS OF 60-70 MPH, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF APPROXIMATELY 50-75 FT.
